summary_one_line: Resolving the name3 pointer-identity check requires a deeply-nested DTD that interns and re-looks-up the same name3 string so dict pointers alias.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that drives the parser deep enough into the DTD entity/element-decl path that xmlHashLookup3 is reached with a dict-interned name3 equal (by pointer) to a stored entry's name3; reaching it at all needs a large, well-formed DOCTYPE structure that survives many parse stages.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer's calibration-derived energy gives faster/smaller/more-stable seeds more havoc mutations per visit, so the FIFO corpus of structured DTD seeds is mutated enough to assemble the deep DOCTYPE that reaches and resolves the lookup (resolved 9/10, hitcount 36).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive runs the same havoc+token stack in a plain StdMutationalStage with no calibrated energy, so it never accumulates the mutation budget on the structured seeds and stays blocked 10/10 with hitcount 0 on the branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: minimizer's CalibrationStage feeds a perf_score-derived per-seed mutation budget (StdPowerMutationalStage) that naive lacks; this calibrated energy is the single-technique delta enabling the winner.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same deep DTD structure reaching xmlHashLookup3 with an aliasing dict-interned name3, requiring sustained mutation pressure on the structured corpus seeds.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: minimizer uses PowerSchedule None (energy from intrinsic calibration only), so the structured DTD seeds keep earning mutations regardless of how often their coverage region was hit, resolving 9/10.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: fast is identical except its FAST AFLFast schedule multiplies energy by an n_fuzz log2 factor that damps frequently-hit (saturated) paths to 0.4x; the hash-lookup path is exercised often, so fast starves the very seeds that could build the deep structure (resolved 2/10, blocked 8/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: the AFLFast rarity power schedule (fast) reweights energy by region hit-frequency and damps the saturated hash-lookup path; minimizer's frequency-agnostic calibrated energy avoids this starvation — the aflfast_rarity delta is what separates them.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid, deeply-nested DTD whose name3 string is reproduced verbatim at the right structural position so the dict interns identical pointers; the byte run must be spliced as a coherent token/gap structure, not a random edit.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: grimoire's GeneralizationStage abstracts corpus DTD bodies into token/gap structures and its Grimoire mutators splice/recurse them, reproducing the well-formed nested DOCTYPE wholesale (resolved 10/10, hitcount 1004, far above any other fuzzer).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S but only havoc+token byte-level mutation; it cannot reconstruct the deep nested structure as a unit and stays blocked 8/10 with hitcount 2.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: grimoire's GeneralizationStage + structural Grimoire mutators (string/gap recombination) rebuild the deep DTD structure that cmplog's byte-level havoc cannot; the grimoire_structural delta is the enabler.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must place the literal name3 byte sequence (e.g. an entity/element name from the DTD) at the offset the parser interns, so that after dict interning the stored entry->name3 and the queried name3 are the same pointer.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog runs I2SRandReplace, which reads the post-execution CMP buffer, finds where one comparison operand appears in the input, and substitutes the matching operand byte-for-byte, directly placing the required name3 literal (resolved 8/10).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace mutator, so it cannot copy the exact operand into the input and must rely on havoc luck, staying blocked 8/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace (present in vpc, absent in value_profile) substitutes the logged CMP operand into the input to satisfy the name comparison feeding the dict-interned name3 identity; this I2S delta is the only difference between the two fuzzers.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must progressively close the gap on the upstream name comparisons (xmlStrEqual / interning compares) so the parser stores then re-queries an identical name3, requiring near-match operand bytes to be retained as corpus.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog adds the CMP_MAP gradient: inputs that produce a CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any seen before are preserved, giving a Hamming/prefix gradient toward the exact name match that cmplog alone does not retain (resolved 8/10).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog has I2S but no CMP_MAP gradient feedback, so partial-match inputs that don't trigger a new edge are discarded; it lacks the distance-bucket signal and stays blocked 8/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: the CMP_MAP gradient feedback (value_profile, present in vpc and absent in plain cmplog) preserves progressively-closer operand matches as corpus entries, enabling convergence on the exact name3 string; this value_profile delta separates the pair.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is a pointer-identity comparison of name3 inside xmlHashLookup3 on the dict path.
The comparison sits on the dict-interned branch, where equality is pointer identity established by dict interning.
The branch is reached via deep DTD parsing (DOCTYPE / entity / attribute-list-decl call chain).
minimizer beats naive on the calibrated_energy delta, resolving 9/10 vs naive 0/10.
minimizer derives per-seed mutation energy from intrinsic calibration which naive lacks.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the energy uses ONLY intrinsic calibration and is NOT weighted by how often
a region has been hit

evidence_trail.claim: naive runs the havoc+token stack in a plain StdMutationalStage with no I2S and no calibrated energy.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: fast is identical to minimizer except the FAST AFLFast power schedule.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
The single-technique delta is therefore `fast`
vs `minimizer`, not vs naive.

evidence_trail.claim: FAST schedule damps saturated paths to 0.4x energy via the n_fuzz factor.
minimizer beats fast on the aflfast_rarity delta (9/10 vs 2/10).
grimoire beats cmplog on the grimoire_structural delta, resolving 10/10 with hitcount 1004.
grimoire adds a GeneralizationStage plus structural Grimoire mutators on top of the cmplog stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
splice and recurse on these generalised token/gap structures
(string-based, grammar-free structural mutation)

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog uses only havoc+token plus I2S, with no structural recombination.
value_profile_cmplog beats value_profile on the I2S delta (8/10 vs 8 blocked).
I2SRandReplace scans the input for an operand and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has the CMP_MAP gradient but no I2SRandReplace mutator.
value_profile_cmplog beats plain cmplog on the value_profile delta (8/10 vs 2/10).
The CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets and preserves inputs that bring operands closer to matching.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The winning grimoire seed contains a complete DOCTYPE/SYSTEM dtd reference structure verbatim.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: No value_profile_cmplog winning seed was bisected for branch 6414 (resolving_seeds holds only grimoire trial-1 seeds), so I could not verify the I2S pair's actual winner. I ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 6414 --role W --fuzzer grimoire --trial 1 --seed b3f5d62fb26f5823`: leaf_mutation_op=I2SRandReplace and a dash row at depth 5, but per the 10-fuzzer caveat grimoire wraps mutators in LineageMutatorWrap with empty names so its dash rows are NOT I2S-exclusive. The literal-named I2SRandReplace leaf corroborates that I2S substitution operates on this branch's seeds, but is not clean direct evidence for the vpc>value_profile pair; I2S contribution may also leak into the havoc bucket.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness isolating the name3 dict-interned identity check where value_profile_cmplog and value_profile resolve at equal rates (no I2S advantage), and where grimoire shows no structural advantage over cmplog, would refute the multi-feature attribution.

weakest_evidence_point: The I2S hypothesis is unverified in lineage because no vpc winning seed was bisected for this branch, so the I2S-vs-CMP_MAP split between Pairs 4 and 5 rests on mechanism reasoning rather than seed-byte evidence.
